concerns.text: The plan cites 'ROI-Parquet (Koch Test)' and 'Koch Classification' (HuggingFace Parquet files) as the primary data sources. However, the spec (FR-001, US-1) and plan (Summary) explicitly require raw fMRI NIfTI files to perform motion correction, normalization, and ROI extraction. Parquet files are tabular and cannot contain raw volumetric fMRI data. The plan assumes these Parquet files contain 'pre-extracted' time-series or that preprocessing can be simulated, but the spec mandates a preprocessing pipeline on raw data. This is a fundamental dataset mismatch: the cited resources do not provide the modality (raw fMRI) required to execute the planned methodology.

The plan states 'Data will be loaded in chunks. fMRI volumes will be masked to ROI masks immediately after normalization'. This workflow is impossible with the cited 'Verified Datasets' (Parquet files) which lack raw NIfTI volumes. The plan must either cite a real fMRI dataset (e.g., OpenNeuro, HCP) containing raw scans or revise the spec to remove the raw preprocessing requirement and rely solely on pre-extracted behavioral/ROI metrics if available in the Parquet schema.
